criteria.description: Pass only when the change introduces no new injection vectors (SQL, command, path, prototype, XSS, template). Every user-controlled value reaching a sink (DB query, shell, fs, HTML, eval/Function, regex from input) is either parameterized, escaped, or validated by a TypeBox schema at the boundary. Fail when raw input is concatenated into a sink, when a TypeBox schema is bypassed, or when a new sink lacks any validation.

Pass only when authentication and authorization paths remain sound. Ory JWT validation, audience/issuer checks, Keto permission checks, and team-scoped diary ownership checks are preserved on every changed route. Fail when an endpoint is added without an explicit auth gate, when a Keto check is removed or weakened, when `optionalAuth` is used where required-auth was needed, or when team/diary scoping is missing on a multi-tenant resource.

Pass only when Ed25519 (or other crypto) usage stays safe: private keys never leave their boundary, signatures are verified before trust, nonces/timestamps prevent replay, and signature payloads are canonical. Fail when a private key is logged or returned over the wire, when verification is skipped or made optional, when a custom crypto primitive is introduced, or when signature input is not canonicalized.

Pass only when no secret material is introduced into source, logs, error messages, or commit history. New env vars are documented and read via the established config layer; PII/JWTs/keys are redacted in Pino logs and OTel attributes. Fail when a token/key/connection string is hardcoded, when an unredacted secret appears in a log statement or error path, or when an env var bypasses dotenvx encryption for a sensitive value.

Pass only when every system-boundary input (HTTP body/query/params, MCP tool args, webhook payload, message queue event) is validated by a TypeBox schema with `additionalProperties: false` and explicit types. Fail when a new boundary handler accepts `unknown`/`any` and threads it inward, when a schema is intentionally loosened, or when validation is moved past the boundary into business logic.

Pass only when new or upgraded dependencies are reputable, minimal, and don't expand the attack surface unnecessarily. Lockfile changes match the package.json edits; no postinstall scripts from unfamiliar packages; no duplicate/shadowed transitive deps that bypass catalog version policy. Fail when a heavyweight dep is added for a trivial need, when a package with known CVEs is pinned, or when `pnpm-workspace.yaml` catalog policy is circumvented.

Pass only when error paths fail closed: failures don't leak stack traces or internal identifiers to clients, retries don't bypass auth, and fallbacks don't silently grant access. Deserialization of untrusted data uses a schema; no `JSON.parse` on attacker-controlled input without subsequent TypeBox validation. Fail when a catch block swallows a security-relevant error, when a fallback path skips an auth/permission check, or when a deserializer is invoked on raw external input.

preamble:
You are performing a security-focused review of a GitHub pull request. Score risk introduced by the diff, not feature correctness or code style. Findings must be actionable and context-aware — complement Semgrep, do not duplicate it. Favor logic and data-flow issues a static analyzer misses.

You may run `gh pr view <n>`, `gh pr diff <n>`, and read files in the workspace. Do not run tests, push commits, or modify anything.

Follow the `security-review` skill if it is advertised in `<available_skills>` — its pipeline (recon → hunt → self-validate → trace → dedup → report) is mandatory, not optional.

When evidence is ambiguous, fail the criterion and explain the ambiguity. Each finding in your PR comment must carry a severity (critical / high / medium / low / info) and a concrete remediation. "Looks fine" is not useful; "the new `/admin/replay` route reads `req.body.token` and calls `verifyEd25519` without checking the JWT audience, allowing cross-tenant replay" is.
